title: Question of Li et al. (2022) on edge density of 3-flow-critical graphs
statement_text: What is the minimum number of edges in an $n$-vertex $3$-flow-critical graph (other than $K_2$ and $K_4$)?
context_text: Li, Ma, Shi, Wang, and Wu studied 3-flow-critical graphs in 2022 and raised the question of determining the minimum edge density of such graphs. The paper under review proves that the answer is at least $\tfrac{5}{3}n$ edges, and that this bound is tight up to lower-order terms, fully answering their question.
attributed_to: Li, Ma, Shi, Wang, and Wu
paper_contribution: Proves that every $n$-vertex $3$-flow-critical graph other than $K_2$ and $K_4$ has at least $\tfrac{5}{3}n$ edges, and that this bound is tight up to lower-order terms, thereby completely answering the question.
notes: The verbatim statement of the question is not reproduced in the provided text; it is reconstructed from the abstract, which states the result 'answers a question of Li et al. (2022)'. Reference [LMS+22]: Li et al., European Journal of Combinatorics, 100:103451, 2022.
